description: Add backend tests for the new trainer dashboard endpoint and the updated admin serializers to ensure batch-module alignment is covered by tests.
steps: Step 1: Add tests in backend/tests/test_trainer.py for the new GET /api/trainer/dashboard endpoint. Test cases: (a) returns correct batch count for the trainer, (b) returns correct student count across trainer's batches, (c) returns correct lecture count, (d) returns upcomingLectures for lectures with date >= today. Follow existing test patterns - use trainer_user/trainer_token fixtures, create Batch + TrainerBatch + Student + Lecture records.

Step 2: Add tests in backend/tests/test_admin.py for the updated GET /api/admin/batches endpoint. Test cases: (a) response includes 'modules' array in each batch, (b) response includes 'trainer' object in each batch, (c) response includes 'studentCount'. Set up BatchModule + TrainerBatch + Student records for the test batch.

Step 3: Add tests in backend/tests/test_admin.py for the updated GET /api/admin/modules endpoint. Test case: response includes 'batches' array in each module. Set up BatchModule record linking module to a batch.
acceptance_criteria: All new tests pass: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -v
New tests actually exercise the new code paths (trainer dashboard endpoint, batch serializer with modules, module serializer with batches)
Tests would fail if the new code were reverted (they assert on the new fields like modules, trainer, studentCount, upcomingLectures)
findings: All 31 tests pass (4 new tests added: TestTrainerDashboard with 2 tests, TestBatchesSerializer with 1 test, TestModulesSerializer with 1 test). Tests verify the new trainer dashboard endpoint returns correct stats and the admin batch/module serializers include modules, trainer, studentCount, status, and batches fields respectively.
